examples.code:
; Hello World Program
; Demonstrates basic output using DOS interrupt 21h

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    msg DB 'Hello, World!$'

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    MOV AH, 09h        ; Function 09h - display string
    LEA DX, msg        ; Load address of message
    INT 21h            ; Call DOS
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch        ; Function 4Ch - terminate
    XOR AL, AL         ; Return code 0
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: String must end with '$' for DOS function 09h
Initialize DS register with data segment address
AH=09h displays the string at DS:DX until '$'
DX points to the message string

examples.code:
; Basic 8086 Program Template
; Standard structure for small model programs

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    ; Data segment - declare variables here
    
.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA      ; Initialize data segment
    MOV DS, AX
    
    ; Your code here
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch        ; Terminate program
    XOR AL, AL
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: .STACK defines stack size (100h = 256 bytes)
@DATA is a special assembler operator
Function 4Ch with AL=0 is the standard exit

examples.code:
; Addition and Negation Operations
; Demonstrates arithmetic instructions and flag effects

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    result DW ?

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    MOV AX, 5         ; AX = 5
    MOV BX, 3         ; BX = 3
    ADD AX, BX        ; AX = AX + BX = 8
    
    ; Store result
    MOV result, AX
    
    ; Negate the result
    NEG AX            ; AX = -AX = -8
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: ADD sets OF, SF, ZF, AF, PF, CF based on result
NEG computes 0 - operand, sets OF, SF, ZF, AF, PF, CF
CF is always set by NEG (except when operand is 0)

commonMistakes: ADD dest, dest (destination cannot be immediate)

examples.code:
; Buffered Keyboard Input
; Reads a string from keyboard using DOS function 0Ah

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    prompt DB 'Enter your name: $'
    buffer DB 80             ; Maximum buffer size
           DB ?              ; Actual size read (filled by DOS)
           DB 80 DUP(?)      ; Buffer for input string

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    ; Display prompt
    MOV AH, 09h
    LEA DX, prompt
    INT 21h
    
    ; Read string
    MOV AH, 0Ah              ; Buffered input function
    LEA DX, buffer
    INT 21h
    
    ; Add CRLF
    MOV AH, 02h
    MOV DL, 0Dh              ; Carriage return
    INT 21h
    MOV DL, 0Ah              ; Line feed
    INT 21h
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: First byte: max characters (including CR)
Second byte: filled by DOS with actual length read
Remaining bytes: the actual string data
Function 0Ah reads until Enter key

examples.code:
; Buffered String Output
; Displays a string using DOS function 09h

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    msg1 DB 'First line$'
    msg2 DB 'Second line$'
    newline DB 0Dh, 0Ah, '$'

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    ; Display first message
    MOV AH, 09h
    LEA DX, msg1
    INT 21h
    
    ; New line
    LEA DX, newline
    INT 21h
    
    ; Display second message
    LEA DX, msg2
    INT 21h
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: newline is 0Dh (CR) + 0Ah (LF) + '$' terminator
Function 09h outputs until '$' delimiter

examples.code:
; Demo Program: Arithmetic and Control Flow
; Shows basic arithmetic and conditional branching

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    num1 DW 10
    num2 DW 20
    result DW ?
    
.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    ; Compare num1 and num2
    MOV AX, num1
    CMP AX, num2
    JE equal
    JL smaller
    
    ; Greater case (fall-through)
    ADD AX, 5
    JMP done
    
smaller:
    SUB AX, 5
    JMP done
    
equal:
    MOV AX, 100
    
done:
    MOV result, AX
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: CMP sets flags but does not modify operands
JE jumps if ZF=1 (num1 == num2)
JL jumps if SF≠OF (num1 < num2)
ADD/SUB modify OF, SF, ZF, AF, PF, CF

examples.code:
; Procedure Example
; Demonstrates procedure definition, CALL/RET, and stack

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    result DW ?

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    ; Call square procedure with parameter in AX
    MOV AX, 5
    CALL square
    MOV result, AX
    
    ; Call add procedure
    PUSH 10         ; First parameter
    PUSH 20         ; Second parameter
    CALL addTwo
    ADD SP, 4       ; Clean up stack (2 params × 2 bytes)
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP

; Squares the value in AX
; Input: AX contains value to square
; Output: AX contains result
square PROC
    PUSH BP
    MOV BP, SP
    MOV AX, [BP+4]   ; Get parameter (offset from BP+4)
    MUL AX           ; AX = AX * AX
    POP BP
    RET             ; Near return
square ENDP

; Adds two parameters
; Input: Stack contains two word parameters
; Output: AX contains sum
addTwo PROC
    PUSH BP
    MOV BP, SP
    MOV AX, [BP+4]   ; First parameter
    ADD AX, [BP+6]   ; Second parameter
    POP BP
    RET
addTwo ENDP

END MAIN

annotations.text: CALL pushes return address and jumps to procedure
PUSH decrements SP by 2 and stores value
Stack frame: [BP+4]=param1, [BP+6]=param2, [BP+2]=return addr
MUL sets OF and CF to 1 if upper half of result is non-zero

examples.code:
; For Loop Example
; Counts from 5 down to 1 using LOOP instruction

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    count DW 5

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    MOV CX, count     ; CX = initial count (LOOP uses CX)
    
next:
    ; Body of loop - do something here
    ; (In real code, you'd output or process)
    
    LOOP next         ; CX--, if CX != 0 jump to next
    
    ; After loop: CX = 0
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: CX must be initialized before LOOP
LOOP decrements CX and jumps if CX ≠ 0
LOOP is atomic - no flag checking needed

examples.code:
; If-Else Example
; Compares two values and branches accordingly

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    val1 DW 25
    val2 DW 30

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    MOV AX, val1
    CMP AX, val2
    JE equal_handler
    JL less_handler
    
    ; Greater handler (AX > val2)
greater_handler:
    ; Handle greater case
    JMP end_comparison
    
less_handler:
    ; Handle less case
    JMP end_comparison
    
equal_handler:
    ; Handle equal case
    
end_comparison:
    ; Continue with program
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: JE (Jump if Equal) is alternate name for JZ
JL tests signed less than (SF ≠ OF)
For unsigned, use JA (above) and JB (below)

commonMistakes: Forgetting to jump past else block

examples.code:
; Exchange Values using XCHG
; Demonstrates swapping register values

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    temp DW ?

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    MOV AX, 1234h     ; AX = 0x1234
    MOV BX, 5678h      ; BX = 0x5678
    
    ; Method 1: Using XCHG (simplest)
    XCHG AX, BX        ; AX and BX are swapped
    
    ; Method 2: Using temp variable
    ; MOV temp, AX
    ; MOV AX, BX
    ; MOV BX, temp
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: XCHG exchanges values of two registers or register and memory
XCHG cannot use immediate values as operands

commonMistakes: Forgetting that XCHG affects no flags

examples.code:
; Compare Instruction
; CMP subtracts operands and sets flags but doesn't store result

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    val1 DW 100
    val2 DW 50

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    MOV AX, val1
    CMP AX, val2
    ; Flags after CMP AX, 50:
    ; ZF = 0 (not equal)
    ; SF = 0 (AX > 50, no sign)
    ; CF = 0 (no borrow needed, AX >= val2)
    
    JE equal          ; Jump if ZF=1
    JNE not_equal     ; Jump if ZF=0
    JL less           ; Jump if SF≠OF (signed less)
    JLE less_equal    ; Jump if SF≠OF or ZF=1
    JG greater        ; Jump if SF=OF and ZF=0
    JGE greater_equal ; Jump if SF=OF
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: CMP is equivalent to SUB but result is discarded
CMP affects: OF, SF, ZF, AF, PF, CF (same as SUB)
JE/JNE are for both signed and unsigned

commonMistakes: Forgetting that CMP doesn't modify destination

examples.code:
; Register Swap Without XCHG
; Shows how to swap values using only MOV

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    temp DW ?

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    MOV AX, 0AAAAh    ; First value
    MOV BX, 0BBBBh    ; Second value
    
    ; Swap using temp storage
    MOV temp, AX      ; temp = AX
    MOV AX, BX        ; AX = BX
    MOV BX, temp      ; BX = temp (= original AX)
    
    ; Now AX = 0BBBBh and BX = 0AAAAh
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: Temp variable needed when XCHG isn't suitable
This approach works with memory operands too

commonMistakes: Overwriting temp before saving first value

examples.code:
; Arithmetic Operations Demo
; Shows various arithmetic instructions and their effects

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    result DW ?

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    ; Addition
    MOV AX, 7
    INC AX              ; AX = 8 (AX = AX + 1)
    ADD AX, 2           ; AX = 10
    
    ; Subtraction  
    DEC AX              ; AX = 9
    SUB AX, 3           ; AX = 6
    
    ; Negation
    NEG AX              ; AX = -6
    
    ; Check flags
    ; After NEG: SF=1 (negative), ZF=0, CF=1, OF depends on operand
    
    MOV result, AX
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: INC/DEC affect OF, SF, ZF, AF, PF but NOT CF
NEG always sets CF=1 (except when operand=0)
NEG sets OF only when operand is MIN_INT

commonMistakes: Using INC on CX in loops (affects LOOP)

examples.code:
; While Loop Example
; Sums numbers from 1 to 10

.MODEL SMALL
.STACK 100h

.DATA
    sum   DW 0
    counter DW 1

.CODE
MAIN PROC
    MOV AX, @DATA
    MOV DS, AX
    
    MOV CX, 10         ; Loop counter (not using LOOP)
    
while_loop:
    CMP CX, 0          ; Check if counter > 0
    JLE end_while      ; Exit if CX <= 0
    
    MOV AX, sum
    ADD AX, counter     ; sum = sum + counter
    MOV sum, AX
    
    DEC counter        ; counter--
    JMP while_loop     ; Continue loop
    
end_while:
    ; sum now contains 55 (1+2+3+...+10)
    
    MOV AH, 4Ch
    INT 21h
MAIN ENDP
END MAIN

annotations.text: While condition checked at top of loop
JLE exits if counter <= 0
Result should be 55 after loop completes

commonMistakes: Infinite loop when condition never becomes false
